profile.name: Jennifer
profile.role_in_household: Primary client and higher earner; sets priorities and pushes for quick, actionable decisions, especially around debt stress.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands investing and taxes in broad strokes, but tends to blur cash-flow details (payment vs balance, gross vs net) when overwhelmed.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Market-risk tolerant and willing to be aggressive with investments, yet becomes short-term cautious when monthly obligations feel tight; prefers solutions that feel like progress without sacrificing lifestyle abruptly.
profile.communication_style: Fast, headline-driven, uses round numbers and fillers like "roughly," "I think," "ballpark." Will interrupt to keep momentum; asks for confirmation when terms get technical.
profile.decision_making_style: Prefers 2–3 options with a recommendation; decides quickly once the plan sounds coherent and doable.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open; trust increases with directness, non-judgment, and clear explanations tied to numbers.
internal_biases: overconfidence in earning her way out of tight periods
recency bias toward current cash-flow pressure
hidden_concerns: fear that debt payments are limiting choices
worry about being behind on retirement despite having accounts
concern about what happens if income becomes less predictable

profile.name: Daniel
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; the numbers-checker who reads statements and notices rates, but often defers to keep discussions calm.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with account types and interest rates; asks clarifying questions and wants assumptions spelled out, but can over-focus on optimization.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More conservative on day-to-day finances; prioritizes liquidity and paying down high-interest debt before taking additional risk, and wants guardrails if investing stays aggressive.
profile.communication_style: Measured and precise; uses "just to confirm" and "if I'm remembering right." Will gently correct figures and ask for the exact rate/term; less talkative until prompted.
emotional_tendencies: frustration with uncertainty and irregular timing
profile.decision_making_style: Methodical and consensus-seeking; prefers to compare scenarios, then commit with checkpoints and a fallback plan.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Skeptical at first; trust is earned through transparency, consistency, and willingness to walk through details.
hidden_concerns: fear of running short on cash despite having investments
concern that fixed debt costs are crowding out flexibility
wants to avoid money conflict in the relationship
